content:
City Traders Imperium Pricing Overview

City Traders Imperium is a South African proprietary trading firm that offers funded forex and indices accounts with a straightforward, evaluation-based pricing model. The firm charges monthly subscription fees starting at $97 per month for its entry-level $10,000 evaluation account. Unlike some prop firms that hide fees behind complex structures, City Traders Imperium presents its costs transparently—you know exactly what you're paying each month and what account size you'll be trading.

Founded in 2018 and based in Cape Town, South Africa, City Traders Imperium has built a reputation for combining affordable evaluation pathways with an integrated trading academy, mentorship programs, and competitive profit splits up to 80%. The company serves intermediate to developing traders who want funded capital without breaking the bank, though the overall cost varies significantly depending on which evaluation tier you choose.

All City Traders Imperium Plans Compared

City Traders Imperium offers four main pricing tiers, each tied to a specific evaluation account size. Here's the complete breakdown:

Evaluation Tier Monthly Cost Annual Cost (Approximate) Account Size Best For
$10K Evaluation $97/mo ~$1,164/year $10,000 funded account Complete beginners; traders testing the platform; risk-averse traders
$25K Evaluation $147/mo ~$1,764/year $25,000 funded account Early-stage traders with some experience; those seeking better risk-reward ratios
$50K Evaluation $247/mo ~$2,964/year $50,000 funded account Intermediate traders; those aiming for sustainable monthly income; serious part-time traders
$100K Evaluation $397/mo ~$4,764/year $100,000 funded account Professional traders; full-time trading ambitions; those targeting $1,000+ monthly income

All four plans grant access to City Traders Imperium's core features, including their integrated trading academy, mentor support, broker integration, automated trading capabilities, performance analytics, and risk management tools. The primary difference between tiers is the amount of capital you're trading with—a critical factor in calculating realistic monthly income potential.

To put this in perspective: if a trader achieves a 5% monthly return on a $10K account, they'd make $500 in profit. But the same 5% return on a $100K account would generate $5,000 in profit. With City Traders Imperium's profit splits reaching 80%, your earnings could range from $400 per month (on the smallest tier) to $4,000+ per month (on the largest), depending on performance.

Free Plan / Free Trial

City Traders Imperium does not offer a free trial or free tier. To access any funded account, you must subscribe to one of the four paid evaluation plans listed above. There is no way to test the platform, trading conditions, or educational materials without paying the monthly subscription.

However, this is not necessarily a disadvantage. Many traders view the monthly subscription as a filtering mechanism—it ensures that only serious, committed traders enter the evaluation process. The firm's website may offer informational materials, webinars, or community engagement that provide some insight into how City Traders Imperium operates, but full access to the platform and funded account requires payment upfront.

Verdict on free trials: If you're uncertain about whether City Traders Imperium is right for you, research their community, watch trader testimonials, and review their trading rules thoroughly before committing. The $97 monthly entry point is relatively low compared to some competitors, making it a reasonable "trial" investment if you're serious about prop trading.

Hidden Costs and Fees

City Traders Imperium's pricing structure is refreshingly transparent compared to many prop firms. The monthly subscription covers most core features, but there are a few additional costs to be aware of:

  • Broker and Exchange Fees: While your monthly subscription includes platform access and mentorship, your forex and indices trades will incur standard broker spreads and commissions. These are typical across the industry and vary based on your broker partner. City Traders Imperium doesn't charge extra for this, but it's factored into your overall trading costs.
  • Data Feeds: Real-time market data is typically included, but premium charting tools or advanced data subscriptions (if available) may carry additional costs. This varies by tier.
  • Withdrawal or Account Transition Fees: If you transition from evaluation to a direct funding account or withdraw profits, there may be processing fees. These aren't always advertised upfront and should be clarified directly with the firm.
  • Mentorship Premium Tiers: While basic mentorship is included, access to elite mentors or one-on-one coaching may require additional payment beyond the base subscription.
  • No Hidden Evaluation Fees: To City Traders Imperium's credit, they don't typically charge extra "challenge fees" or hidden evaluation costs once you've paid your monthly subscription. Your funded account is active for that month.

The transparency around these fees is one of City Traders Imperium's stated strengths—"transparent trading rules with no hidden restrictions" is listed as a key pro. However, always confirm specific fee structures directly with their support team before subscribing.

City Traders Imperium Pricing vs Competitors

To understand whether City Traders Imperium offers good value, here's how their pricing compares to two major competitors in the prop trading space:

Prop Firm Entry Tier Price Mid-Tier Price Highest Tier Price Account Range
City Traders Imperium $97/mo $247/mo $397/mo $10K–$100K
FTMO ~$155/mo (approx.) ~$310/mo (approx.) ~$620/mo (approx.) $10K–$100K
Topstep ~$99/mo (approx.) ~$199/mo (approx.) ~$399/mo (approx.) $5K–$100K+

Key Insights from this comparison:

  • City Traders Imperium is often the cheapest option at entry level. At $97/month for a $10K account, it undercuts FTMO and matches Topstep's pricing in the smallest tier.
  • Mid-tier pricing is competitive. For a $50K account ($247/month), City Traders Imperium is significantly cheaper than FTMO and comparable to Topstep.
  • The value proposition extends beyond pricing. City Traders Imperium's integrated trading academy and mentorship programs give it an edge over purely platform-focused competitors. If education is important to you, the slightly lower price point combined with built-in training makes this firm particularly attractive.
  • Profit splits matter. City Traders Imperium offers up to 80% profit splits. FTMO typically offers 80-90%, while Topstep ranges from 50-90% depending on the tier. City Traders Imperium is competitive here.
  • Market limitations. City Traders Imperium focuses primarily on forex and indices, which is narrower than some competitors. If you want access to crypto, commodities, or US equities, other firms may be better suited.

Is City Traders Imperium Worth the Price?

Yes, for the right trader. City Traders Imperium represents good value if you meet these criteria:

  • You're developing your forex/indices trading skills and want mentorship alongside capital. The integrated academy and community support justify the monthly cost.
  • You're budget-conscious but serious about prop trading. At $97/month for $10K, it's one of the most affordable funded account options available.
  • You value community and education. If you learn well from peer traders and structured courses, City Traders Imperium's "strong community culture" and trading academy make it stand out from competitors focused purely on execution.
  • You prefer flexible funding pathways. City Traders Imperium offers both evaluation-based accounts and direct funding options, giving you choices if you already have a trading history.
  • You're trading forex and indices exclusively. The firm's strengths lie in these markets. If you need multi-asset support, look elsewhere.

When to upgrade tiers: Upgrade from the $10K to $25K plan once you've consistently proven profitability on the smaller account (typically after 2-3 months of positive results). Move to $50K when you're comfortable with $500+ monthly income targets. Jump to $100K only when you're approaching $1,000+ monthly goals and have significant trading experience.

When City Traders Imperium might not be worth it: If you're a complete beginner with no trading background, the $97/month subscription is an additional cost on top of the learning curve. If you want to trade crypto, US equities, or exotic instruments, you'll need to look elsewhere.

How to Save on City Traders Imperium

City Traders Imperium's pricing is already competitive, but here are legitimate ways to reduce costs:

  • Start with the $10K tier. At $97/month, the entry-level account is the most cost-effective way to test the platform and prove your trading edge. You can always scale up once profitable.
  • Annual billing discounts: Check with City Traders Imperium directly about annual subscription discounts. Many prop firms offer 10-20% off if you commit to a full year upfront. This could save $100-200+ annually.
  • Leverage the community and academy. Your monthly fee includes access to mentorship and trading courses. Maximize this value by actively engaging with other traders and completing the academy modules—you're already paying for it.
  • Evaluate profitability before upgrading. Don't pay $247/month for a $50K account if you're not consistently profitable on the $10K tier. Each tier jump should be justified by proven trading results.
  • Watch for promotional periods. Around major trading events or quarter-end, prop firms often run limited-time promotions. Monitor City Traders Imperium's website and social channels for discounts or special offers.
  • Referral bonuses: Ask if City Traders Imperium offers referral commissions. If you bring in new traders, you might earn credits toward your monthly subscription.

Bottom line: City Traders Imperium's pricing is transparent and competitive, especially when you factor in their educational offerings and community support. For developing traders seeking affordable funded accounts with mentorship, it's a solid choice. Start with the $10K tier, prove your edge, and scale responsibly.

For more details on City Traders Imperium features and trader reviews, visit our full review page. To compare this firm with other top-tier prop trading platforms, check our prop firm comparison guide.
